input.background: Table 19.3 contains data on the acceptance of applications to graduate programs at the University of California Berkeley for 1973. Assuming men and women who applied for admission were equally well-qualified, one would expect an equal proportion of acceptance by gender.
input.question: Explain why the crude analysis (part a) produced a faulty conclusion.

output.answer: The initial analysis was confounded because males tended to apply to major 1. Major 1 had a high acceptance rate (601 of 933 = 64%), while major 2 had a low acceptance rate (46 of 714 = 6%).

Acceptance_Status.description: The acceptance status of the applicants.

Applicants_Combined_Major.description: Number of applicants by gender for both majors combined.

Applicants_Major_1.description: Number of applicants by gender for Major 1.

Applicants_Major_2.description: Number of applicants by gender for Major 2.

meta_info.caption: Sex bias in graduate school admissions?
